publicSideBand: City of Newark replaces from the main line to the meter free of charge
privateSideBand: City of Newark says the service line replacement is free of charge to the homeowner through the current program
fullReplacementBand: Full replacement from main to meter is free of charge in the current grant-funded program
housingTypeAssumption: Newark's current program language applies to eligible noticed properties and signup flow
permitAssumption: Eligibility depends on program notices and replacement map status
restorationAssumption: City-funded restoration of disturbed areas is included in the current program
methodologySummary: Newark's current cost page is grounded in the city's grant-funded promise to replace the full line from the main to the meter and restore disturbed areas without charging the homeowner.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners avoid direct cost only when the property is in the city's current replacement workflow and the owner follows the notice and sign-up path instead of arranging a separate private replacement.
